children.heading: Boundary revision
children.label: a Boundary revision
children.text: The Salem Maritime National Historic Site (hereafter in this section referred to as the “national historic site”), designated on , under (), and located in Salem, Massachusetts, shall consist of lands and interests in lands as generally depicted on the map entitled “Boundary Map, Salem Maritime National Historic Site, Salem, Massachusetts”, numbered 373–80,011, and dated April 1987. The map shall be on file and available for public inspection in the offices of the National Park Service, Department of the Interior.1March 17, 1938section 2 of the Act of August 21, 193549 Stat. 666

The Secretary of the Interior may acquire lands or interests therein within the boundary of the national historic site by donation, purchase with donated or appropriated funds, or exchange. Any lands or interests in lands owned by the Commonwealth of Massachusetts or any political subdivision thereof may be acquired only by donation. Lands and interests therein acquired pursuant to this section shall become part of the national historic site and shall be subject to all the laws and regulations applicable to the national historic site.111

children.label: A
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the Secretary may acquire under this subsection property or an interest therein in the city of Salem for use as a visitor center or the national historic site. The Secretary shall conduct an economic analysis of the costs and benefits of acquiring such property or interest therein. Funds appropriated for the development and operation of the visitor center may be expended on property in which the Secretary has acquired less than a fee simple interest.1

children.label: I
children.text: under any lease, the leased period shall not be less than 25 years and shall contain an option to renew for an addtional 25 years;2So in original. Probably should be “additional”.2

children.label: II
children.text: the owner of the property shall maintain the property to a standard acceptable to the Secretary;

children.label: III
children.text: under any lease, rental amounts paid by the Secretary may not exceed the fair market value of the leased premises, as determined by an independent party acceptable to both the lessor and the Secretary; and

children.label: IV
children.text: under any lease, rental payments be reduced by the fair market value of improvements in the leased premises made by or at the expense of the Secretary.

children.label: ii
children.text: The Secretary may not acquire an interest in more than 12,000 square feet under this paragraph.

children.label: iii
children.text: The Secretary shall submit the economic analysis, together with any proposed acquisition, to the appropriate committees of Congress for their review at least 120 days before the effective date of such acquisition.
